frames: The agent stands upright, slightly leaning forward with arms bent at the elbows and held near its chest.
It begins to lose balance, leaning further forward and to its right, with its left leg lifting slightly.
The agent continues to fall forward and to its right, with its body significantly angled and left leg extended backward.
Its upper body is almost parallel to the ground, falling rapidly with arms bent and legs splayed.
The agent makes contact with the ground, landing on its chest and arms, with legs still somewhat extended.
It is now lying prone on the ground, with its upper body supported by its forearms and legs bent at the knees.
The agent's legs begin to curl up towards its body, while its upper body remains in a prone position.
The agent is fully collapsed on the ground, lying on its side with limbs curled, appearing motionless.
